attractions.name: Albany County Courthouse
attractions.description: The Albany County Courthouse is a prominent historic landmark in Laramie, serving as the seat of county government and the judicial system. Designed by architects Wilbur Hitchcock and William Dubois and completed in 1932, the building stands on the site of the original 1889 courthouse. It reflects the civic architectural style of the early 20th century and continues to house vital county offices, including the District Court and County Clerk. (WyoHistory.org, Visit Laramie)

attractions.admission: Free (Albany County, WY)
attractions.phone: (307) 721-2541 (County Clerk), (307) 721-2508 (District Court) (Albany County, WY, Wyoming Judicial Branch)
highlights: 'Designed by renowned Laramie architect Wilbur Hitchcock and William Dubois in 1931. ([UWYO)', 'Constructed in 1931-1932 to replace the original 1889 Romanesque Revival building. (WyoHistory.org)', 'Houses the Second Judicial District Court, Circuit Court, and Albany County administrative offices. (Wyoming Judicial Branch)', 'Features a significant World War I Memorial nearby at the corner of 6th Street and Ivinson Avenue. (WyoHistory.org)', 'Underwent major renovations in 2021 to improve accessibility and add a new north entrance. (CaFE)']
attractions.tips: Visitors can admire the historic architecture from the public sidewalks and explore the various historic markers surrounding the building. As it is an active judicial facility, visitors should expect security screenings upon entry and should check for public court sessions or administrative hours if visiting for official business. (Paranormal Traveler)

attractions.name: American Heritage Center, University of Wyoming
attractions.description: The American Heritage Center (AHC) is the University of Wyoming's premier repository for manuscripts, rare books, and university archives, recognized as one of the largest non-governmental archives in the nation. Housed in the iconic Antoine Predock-designed Centennial Complex, it preserves extensive collections on Wyoming and Western history, along with national topics like entertainment, conservation, and journalism. The center serves as both a research facility and a public museum, offering rotating exhibits that highlight the history and culture of the Rocky Mountain West.
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Thursday: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Friday: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['One of the largest non-governmental archives in the United States, housing over 90,000 cubic feet of material.', 'The Toppan Rare Books Library, which contains more than 50,000 rare volumes and manuscripts.', 'Iconic architecture by Antoine Predock, featuring a distinctive black copper "archival mountain" cone aligned with the landscape.', 'Extensive 20th-century entertainment collections, including the papers of Stan Lee, Jack Benny, and Barbara Stanwyck.', 'Significant holdings in Western history, environmental conservation, and the mining and petroleum industries.', 'Public gallery spaces featuring rotating historical and archival exhibits open to all visitors.']
attractions.tips: Researchers are encouraged to schedule an appointment at least 24 hours in advance to access collection materials in the Reading Room. Admission and visitor parking at the Centennial Complex are free. The center follows the University of Wyoming academic calendar, so visitors should check for holiday or semester break closures.

attractions.name: Bamforth National Wildlife Refuge
attractions.description: Established in 1932, the Bamforth National Wildlife Refuge encompasses 1,166 non-contiguous acres of mixed-grass uplands and wetland habitats in the Laramie Plains. It serves as a vital resting and feeding area for migrating waterfowl and songbirds in a semiarid high plains basin ecosystem. The refuge is managed as part of the Central Sage Steppe Conservation Complex and is designated as an Important Bird Area.
attractions.address: 953 Jackson County Road 32, Walden, CO 80480 (Administrative Office); Physical: Northwest of Laramie off US-287 North
attractions.hours: Daily, 1/2 hour before sunrise to 1/2 hour after sunset
attractions.admission: Free (Closed to general public; hunting requires appropriate permits and licenses)
highlights: ['Critical habitat for migrating waterfowl and shorebirds', 'Important Bird Area (IBA) designation by Wyoming Audubon', 'Diverse habitats including alkali flats and greasewood grasslands', 'Part of the Central Sage Steppe Conservation Complex', 'Excellent opportunities for perimeter birdwatching and photography']
attractions.tips: The refuge is closed to most public access except for designated hunting seasons; however, visitors can observe wildlife from public roads or nearby state lands. Bring high-powered binoculars or a spotting scope for birdwatching from the periphery, and dress in layers for unpredictable Wyoming weather. Always check the official FWS site for current hunting regulations and seasonal closures.

attractions.name: Curt Gowdy State Park (Wyoming State Parks)
attractions.description: Curt Gowdy State Park is a public recreation area covering 3,395 acres in Albany and Laramie counties, named after the legendary broadcaster and Wyoming native. The park features three reservoirs—Granite Springs, Crystal, and North Crow—and is renowned for its world-class 35-mile trail system that caters to hikers, mountain bikers, and equestrians alike. Visitors can enjoy a diverse landscape of rolling hills, low-lying meadows, and dramatic granite formations (Wikipedia, Wyoming State Parks).
attractions.region: Laramie Mountains, Albany and Laramie Counties (Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Park: 24 hours daily; Visitor Center (Summer Hours): Mon-Thu 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Fri-Sat 9:00 AM - 7:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Wyoming State Parks)
attractions.admission: Residents: $7 per day per vehicle; Non-residents: $12 per day per vehicle; Children 17 and under: Free (Wyoming State Parks)
highlights: 'Extensive 35-mile trail system recognized for world-class mountain biking ([Travel Wyoming).', 'Three distinct reservoirs (Granite, Crystal, and North Crow) for fishing and boating (Wyoming State Parks).', 'Hynds Lodge, a historic site listed on the National Register of Historic Places (Wikipedia).', "Scenic Hidden Falls, reachable via the park's trail network (Wikipedia).", 'State-of-the-art, green-certified visitor center providing maps and local information (Wikipedia).', 'Abundant wildlife viewing opportunities including mule deer and various bird species (Wyoming State Parks).']
attractions.tips: The park does not have a trailer dump station; the nearest free station is at the Southeast Wyoming Welcome Center in Cheyenne. Due to the high altitude, visitors should use sunscreen and stay well-hydrated. Wyoming weather can change rapidly, so it is recommended to bring layers and check forecasts before arrival (Wyoming State Parks, Visit Cheyenne).

Known for its iconic neon pole sign, the motel served travelers for decades before being converted into studio and one-bedroom apartments. Today, it remains a local landmark, with its historic sign still standing as a tribute to Laramie's mid-20th-century travel history (RoadsideArchitecture.com, Albany County Historical Society).

attractions.name: Wyoming Union
attractions.description: The Wyoming Union serves as the central hub and "campus living room" for the University of Wyoming community, offering a variety of dining, shopping, and student services. Originally built in 1939 and renovated extensively in 2000, it provides a vibrant atmosphere for students, faculty, and visitors to study, socialize, and engage in campus life. Wikipedia, UW Website
attractions.region: University of Wyoming Campus / Albany County
attractions.hours: Summer Hours (May-August): Monday-Friday: 7:00 am - 7:00 pm, Saturday-Sunday: Closed. Regular Academic Hours: Monday-Friday: 7:00 am - 11:00 pm (estimated), Saturday: 10:00 am - 6:30 pm (estimated), Sunday: Closed (most services) UW Rec, Evendo
attractions.days_open: Monday-Friday (Summer); Monday-Saturday (Academic Year) Chick-fil-A Hours, UW Rec Hours
attractions.admission: Free (Open to the public and campus visitors) UW Website, UW Exchange
highlights: 'Houses popular dining options including Chick-fil-A, Panda Express, and Starbucks [UW Website', 'Home to the University Store, the official retailer for UW apparel, textbooks, and memorabilia University Store', 'Features Pokes Pub and a Game Room with air hockey, pool, and an E-Sports lounge UW Exchange', 'Central location for student organizations, including the Center for Student Involvement & Leadership (CSIL) UW Website', 'Historic 1939 stone architecture that has evolved through multiple renovations to meet modern student needs Wikipedia', 'The "Union Breezeway" acts as the campus "Main Street," connecting various services and student hubs UW Website']
attractions.tips: Check the Union Information Desk on the main floor for lost items or general campus directions. Metered parking is available nearby but is monitored from 8 am to 5 pm on weekdays. It is a great starting point for a campus walking tour or a quick caffeine break between lectures. UW Information Desk, UW Website
attractions.nearby: Prexy’s Pasture, UW Geological Museum, Half Acre Recreation and Wellness Center, Arena-Auditorium, War Memorial Stadium UW Website, JE Dunn

attractions.name: Yellow Pine Campground (Happy Jack Recreation Area)
attractions.description: Yellow Pine Campground is a scenic recreation area located within the Happy Jack Recreation Area of the Medicine Bow National Forest. It features 19 campsites nestled among stands of mixed conifers and aspen, serving as a popular base for outdoor enthusiasts. The area provides direct access to the extensive Tie City trail system, which is used for hiking and mountain biking in the summer and cross-country skiing in the winter. (USDA Forest Service, Visit Laramie)
attractions.region: Medicine Bow National Forest / Happy Jack Recreation Area
attractions.hours: Open year-round for day use; camping is available seasonally from approximately June to October (USDA Forest Service, Campflare)
attractions.admission: $5.00 per vehicle per day for day use; $20.00 per night for camping (USDA Forest Service, Recreation.gov)
highlights: 'Access to the Tie City trail system for mountain biking and hiking ([Recreation.gov)', 'Scenic views of the surrounding hills and mixed conifer/aspen forests (The Dyrt)', 'Equestrian corrals available outside the campground loop (USDA Forest Service)', 'Brook trout fishing in the nearby South Fork of Lodgepole Creek (USDA Forest Service)', 'Winter recreation including groomed cross-country skiing and sledding (Visit Laramie)']
attractions.tips: There is no potable water available at the campground, so visitors must bring their own supply. Dogs must be kept on a leash at all times, and quiet hours are enforced from 10:00 PM to 8:00 AM. (Recreation.gov, The Dyrt)
attractions.nearby: Vedauwoo Recreation Area, Tie City Campground, Summit Visitor Center, Ames Monument, Curt Gowdy State Park (Visit Laramie, Recreation.gov)

attractions.name: Ames Monument State Historic Site
attractions.description: The Ames Monument is a 60-foot granite pyramid designed by Henry Hobson Richardson and completed in 1882 to honor Oakes and Oliver Ames, key financiers of the Union Pacific Railroad. It marks the highest point of the original transcontinental railroad route at an elevation of 8,247 feet (Wyoming State Parks). The monument is a National Historic Landmark and stands as a striking example of Richardsonian Romanesque architecture on the Wyoming prairie (WyoHistory.org).
attractions.hours: Mon: Sunrise - Sunset, Tue: Sunrise - Sunset, Wed: Sunrise - Sunset, Thu: Sunrise - Sunset, Fri: Sunrise - Sunset, Sat: Sunrise - Sunset, Sun: Sunrise - Sunset (Wyoming State Parks)
attractions.admission: Free (Wyoming State Parks)
highlights: 'Marks the highest elevation on the original First Transcontinental Railroad (8,247 feet) ([Wikipedia)', 'Designed by Henry Hobson Richardson, one of the most influential American architects of the 19th century (National Park Service)', 'Features 9-foot-tall bas-relief medallions of the Ames brothers by renowned sculptor Augustus Saint-Gaudens (Wyoming State Parks)', 'Constructed from native "Sherman granite" quarried just half a mile from the site (WyoHistory.org)', 'Designated as a National Historic Landmark in 2016 for its historical and architectural significance (Visit Laramie)', 'Located at the site of the former railroad town of Sherman, which is now a ghost town (Wyoming State Parks)']
attractions.tips: The site is located on a treeless prairie and is often extremely windy; visitors should dress for the elements (Visit Laramie). Visit during sunrise or sunset for the most dramatic lighting on the granite structure (Evendo). Note that the interior passages of the monument are sealed and not accessible to the public (Cowboy State Daily).
attractions.nearby: Vedauwoo Recreation Area, Abraham Lincoln Memorial Monument (Lincoln Bust), Tree-in-the-Rock, Curt Gowdy State Park, and Laramie Territorial Prison State Historic Site (Visit Laramie)

attractions.name: Chimney Park (includes Chimney Park Scout Camp and Trailhead)
attractions.description: Chimney Park is a high-altitude recreation area within the Medicine Bow-Routt National Forest, located approximately 40 miles west of Laramie. It is renowned for its 15-mile network of groomed classic Nordic ski trails in the winter and offers mountain biking, wildlife viewing, and trout fishing in the Chimney Park Reservoir during the summer (Visit Laramie, USDA Forest Service).
attractions.region: Medicine Bow-Routt National Forest, Albany County
attractions.hours: Trails and recreation area: 24/7; Trailhead grooming: Limited weekends in winter; Scout camp: By reservation (USDA Forest Service, SNOW)
attractions.admission: Free entry to the forest; $5 per vehicle for day-use parking at the trailhead (USDA Forest Service, Longs Peak Council)
highlights: '15 miles of groomed trails dedicated to classic Nordic skiing and snowshoeing ([Visit Laramie)', 'Chimney Park Reservoir, a popular spot for trout fishing and non-motorized boating (Scouter Forum)', 'The historic Baldwin Lodge, which is available for group reservations and events (Longs Peak Council)', 'Three main summer mountain biking loops: Jelm, Lodgepole, and Porter (Singletracks)', 'Frequent wildlife sightings, including elk, deer, and diverse bird species (USDA Forest Service)', 'Dense lodgepole pine forests offering a serene and remote outdoor experience (Visit Laramie)']
attractions.tips: There is no potable water on-site, so visitors should bring their own supply; cell service is extremely limited or non-existent; high-altitude conditions (over 9,000 feet) mean weather can change rapidly (USDA Forest Service, Visit Laramie).
attractions.nearby: Woods Landing, Jelm Mountain, Medicine Bow Rail Trail, Snowy Range Scenic Byway (Visit Laramie, Evendo)

attractions.name: Brooklyn Lodge
attractions.description: Brooklyn Lodge is a National Historic Place built in 1922–23 by rodeo performer Harry 'Hoot' Jones. Located in the Medicine Bow National Forest at an elevation of 10,200 feet, this historic log lodge offers a rustic mountain retreat featuring a full-length covered porch and a large stone fireplace. It currently operates as a seasonal bed and breakfast, vacation rental, and idyllic wedding venue surrounded by meadows and trout streams.
attractions.region: Snowy Range Mountains, Medicine Bow National Forest
attractions.hours: Check-in: 4:00 PM; Breakfast: 9:00 AM; Open Daily (Late June – September)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Seasonal: Last week of June through September)
attractions.admission: Bed & Breakfast: $295 per night; Full Lodge Rental: $700–$790 per night; Wedding Venue: $3,800 per event; Cash or checks only
highlights: ['Listed on the National Register of Historic Places for its significance in early dude ranching history.', 'Spectacular wildlife viewing opportunities, including frequent sightings of moose, deer, and elk in the front meadow.', 'Features a historic Western-inspired main hall with a large stone fireplace and hand-hewn log furniture.', 'Located just off the Snowy Range Scenic Byway, providing easy access to pristine alpine landscapes.', 'Expansive covered porch with rockers and a swing, perfect for watching sunrises over the Laramie Valley.', 'Proximity to two babbling trout streams and several high-altitude glacial lakes.']
attractions.tips: Reservations should be made up to a year in advance as it books quickly; be prepared for limited cell service and high altitude; bring layers for unpredictable mountain weather.
attractions.nearby: Brooklyn Lake, Medicine Bow Peak, Snowy Range Ski Area, Nici Self Historical Museum, St. Alban’s Chapel

attractions.name: Libby Flats Observation Site
attractions.description: Libby Flats Observation Point is the highest point on the Snowy Range Scenic Byway, sitting at an elevation of 10,847 feet. It features a unique stone observation tower reminiscent of a fortress that provides 360-degree panoramic views of the Snowy Range, the Sierra Madre Mountains, and the Rocky Mountains in Colorado. The site is a popular stop for photography, stargazing, and observing the high-altitude alpine ecosystem of the Medicine Bow-Routt National Forest. Visit Laramie, USDA Forest Service
attractions.address: 160-194 WY-130 (Snowy Range Scenic Byway), Centennial, WY 82055
attractions.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours, Tuesday: Open 24 hours, Wednesday: Open 24 hours, Thursday: Open 24 hours, Friday: Open 24 hours, Saturday: Open 24 hours, Sunday: Open 24 hours (Note: Seasonal access is dependent on the opening of Highway 130, typically May/June to October) Airial Travel, Evendo
attractions.days_open: Daily (Seasonal: late May/June through October) USDA Forest Service
attractions.admission: $5 per vehicle per day; $30 annual pass USDA Forest Service
highlights: ['360-degree panoramic views of the Snowy Range and distant Rocky Mountains', 'Unique "castle-like" stone observation tower structure', 'Highest point on the Snowy Range Scenic Byway at 10,847 feet', 'Short, accessible paved interpretive trail leading to the overlook', 'Exceptional dark-sky stargazing opportunities away from city lights', 'High-altitude alpine tundra ecosystem with unique summer wildflowers']
attractions.tips: Prepare for high-altitude weather by dressing in layers, as winds are strong and temperatures are significantly cooler than in Laramie. Bring binoculars for a better view of distant peaks and local wildlife like yellow-bellied marmots. Visit between July and August to ensure the highway is fully clear of snow and wildflowers are in bloom. Anne's Travels, Visit Laramie
attractions.nearby: Medicine Bow Peak, Lake Marie, Mirror Lake, Miner's Cabin Trail, Centennial Visitor Center, Snowy Range Ski Area Visit Laramie

attractions.name: Medicine Bow Peak Trail
attractions.description: Medicine Bow Peak is the highest point in the Snowy Range and southern Wyoming, reaching an elevation of 12,018 feet. The trail offers a stunning alpine experience with 360-degree panoramic views, vibrant wildflower meadows, and crystal-clear glacial lakes. Wikipedia, Visit Laramie
attractions.address: Snowy Range Scenic Byway (Highway 130), Centennial, WY 82055 Forest Service
attractions.region: Snowy Range, Medicine Bow National Forest
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily during the summer season (typically late May to mid-November, depending on snow levels and road closures) Visit Laramie, Forest Service
attractions.admission: $5 Day-Use Fee at select trailheads (e.g., Lewis Lake/Sugarloaf); Free at other access points like West Lake Marie Forest Service, Visit Laramie
highlights: 'Summit reaching 12,018 feet with 360-degree panoramic views [Wikipedia', 'Crystal-clear alpine lakes including Lake Marie, Mirror Lake, and Lewis Lake Visit Laramie', 'Snowy Range Scenic Byway (Highway 130) access Forest Service', 'Abundant summer wildflowers and high-altitude meadows Visit Laramie', 'Historical site of a 1950s commercial airliner crash The Adventuring Heidi', 'Vibrant geological formations and rugged quartzite ridges Wikipedia']
attractions.tips: Arrive at trailheads before 8:00 AM to secure parking, especially on weekends. Be prepared for sudden weather changes and plan to be off the summit by noon to avoid frequent afternoon thunderstorms. Trekking poles are highly recommended for the rocky switchbacks and potential snowfields. The Adventuring Heidi, Live and Let Hike
attractions.nearby: Lake Marie, Mirror Lake, Libby Flats Overlook, Snowy Range Scenic Byway, Town of Centennial Visit Laramie

attractions.name: C85 Branding Iron (Note: Primarily located in Casper, WY)
attractions.description: The Branding Iron is a vibrant gourmet burger restaurant and full-service bar located in the heart of downtown Casper, Wyoming. Known for its family-friendly atmosphere and unique Western-themed culinary creations, it features a quaint outdoor patio and large garage doors that open to the local surroundings. Visit Casper, Wanderlog
attractions.address: 129 W 2nd St, Casper, WY 82601 C85 Group, Visit Casper (Note: Located in Casper, 2 hours from Laramie)
attractions.hours: Sunday - Wednesday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Thursday - Saturday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM C85 Group, Wanderlog
attractions.admission: Free (No cover charge) Tripadvisor
highlights: 'Gourmet burgers featuring beef, bison, and yak options [Wanderlog', 'Full-service bar with a diverse selection of refreshing beers Visit Casper', 'Outdoor patio and garage doors for an open-air dining experience C85 Group', 'Signature dishes like the Kona Burger and Pork Belly Mac & Cheese Wyoming Office of Tourism', 'Daily happy hour specials in a dedicated lounge area C85 Group', "Family-friendly atmosphere with a specialized 'Lil Buckaroos' menu Wanderlog"]
attractions.tips: Try the specialty burgers like the Kona (with teriyaki and grilled pineapple) or the bison patty upgrade. Be sure to sample the steak bites appetizer and save room for the homemade cheesecake. Wanderlog, Wyoming Office of Tourism
attractions.nearby: David Street Station, Nicolaysen Art Museum, Lou Taubert Royal West Visit Casper

attractions.name: Carbon County Museum (Official Site)
attractions.description: The Carbon County Museum preserves and interprets the rich heritage of south-central Wyoming, featuring over 30,000 artifacts and archives. Its diverse exhibits cover Native American cultures, the impact of the Union Pacific Railroad, and the region's pioneering and industrial history (Wonderful Museums, Travel Wyoming). It is famously known for housing the macabre remains of the outlaw "Big Nose" George Parrott (Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 904 West Walnut Street, Rawlins, WY 82301 (Official Site)
attractions.hours: Tuesday-Saturday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Sunday-Monday: Closed (Official Site)
attractions.days_open: Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday (Official Site)
attractions.admission: Adults: $6, Children (Ages 3-15): $4, Seniors (65+): $3, Veterans: $2, Children Under 3: Free (Official Site)
highlights: 'Shoes made from the skin of the outlaw "Big Nose" George Parrott and his earless death mask ([Wikipedia)', "The Dr. Lillian Heath exhibit, dedicated to Wyoming's first female physician (WyoHistory)", "One of the nation's largest exhibits dedicated to the history of the Union Pacific Railroad (Tripadvisor)", '"The Garage" featuring a 1920s LaFrance fire truck, an authentic sheep wagon, and a 1920s Hudson Essex racecar (Official Site)', 'Extensive collections on Indigenous Peoples of Carbon County, including Shoshone and Arapaho artifacts (Official Site)', "Exhibits on Thomas Edison's visit to the region and his research related to the incandescent light bulb (AAA)"]
attractions.tips: Plan to spend 1-2 hours to see all exhibits (Tripadvisor). Families can take advantage of the children's scavenger hunt to keep kids engaged (Tripadvisor). Call ahead for guided group tours or research requests (Official Site).
attractions.nearby: Wyoming Frontier Prison (0.3 mi), Downtown Rawlins Historic District, Tully Park, Sinclair Historic District, Seminoe State Park (Tripadvisor, Great American West)

attractions.name: Centennial Village Museum (City of Greeley)
attractions.description: Centennial Village Museum is an 8-acre living history experience that transports visitors back to the settling of the western high plains from the 1860s to the 1940s. The site features over 35 historic structures, costumed interpreters, and heritage farm animals to preserve American western heritage (City of Greeley, Visit Greeley).
attractions.address: 1475 A Street, Greeley, CO 80631 (City of Greeley)
attractions.hours: Summer Season (late May – August): Saturday - Sunday, 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Closed Monday - Tuesday; Wednesday - Friday by prior booking for group visits (City of Greeley)
attractions.days_open: Saturday - Sunday (Summer Season); Wednesday - Friday (Group Visits only) (City of Greeley)
attractions.admission: Free for regular admission; fees apply for group visits and special events (e.g., $8 adults, $5 youth for specific programs) (City of Greeley, Expedia)
highlights: 'Over 35 historic buildings including a 1910 Union Pacific Depot ([SAH Archipedia)', 'Live demonstrations of blacksmithing and woodcarving (City of Greeley)', 'Heritage farm animals and heirloom gardens (Visit Greeley)', 'Annual festivals such as Potato Day and Baby Animal Days (K99)', 'Costumed interpreters sharing stories of early settlers (Colorado Parent)']
attractions.tips: Scan the QR codes throughout the site or call the designated phone number for a self-guided audio tour. Plan your visit around annual special events like 'Pets ‘N Popsicles' in August for kid-friendly animal interactions (City of Greeley, Colorado Parent).
attractions.nearby: Island Grove Regional Park, Greeley History Museum, Colorado Model Railroad Museum (Colorado.com)

attractions.name: Monolith Brewing
attractions.description: Monolith Brewing is a sustainability-focused nanobrewery located in Denver's SoBo neighborhood that prides itself on using 100% locally grown and malted Colorado barley, wheat, and yeast. The taproom features a community-driven atmosphere with unique murals, creative lighting, and a commitment to environmental responsibility, including a paperless operation and in-house composting (Monolith Brewing, Westword).
attractions.region: SoBo (South Broadway) / Platt Park (In The Loop Travel)
attractions.hours: Sunday - Monday: 12 PM to 9 PM; Tuesday - Saturday: 12 PM to 10 PM (Monolith Brewing)
attractions.admission: Free (Colorado Brewery List)
highlights: 'Sustainability-focused operation with a paperless taproom and in-house composting ([Monolith Brewing)', 'Brews exclusively with 100% locally grown and malted Colorado grain and yeast (Shoutout Colorado)', 'Features unique entertainment like feather bowling and community events such as live jazz jams (Westword, Yelp)', 'Donates 1% of all revenue to the Brew Like a Girl Scholarship Fund for women in brewing science (Colorado Brewery List)', 'Serves a diverse lineup of craft beers alongside wine, cocktails, and non-alcoholic options (Denver Curated)']
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to bring in food or order empanadas from the neighboring Maria Empanada, which are reheated fresh in the taproom. Be sure to check out the weekly Tuesday night jazz jam or Thursday music bingo for a lively neighborhood vibe (Westword, Monolith Brewing).
attractions.nearby: Maria Empanada, Platt Park, South Broadway Shopping District (Westword, Yelp)
